intent:
Produce an Enumeration / Catalog report cataloguing every small business loan available through the United States Small Business Administration, including SBA loan types, terms, and eligibility criteria. The report is NOT a recommendation guide; it is a comprehensive taxonomy of available loan options.

Ground in >= 120 sandbox URLs (>= 60 cited). (A) `__SHOPPING__` >= 40 SBA loan product pages — cite loan type + terms + eligibility requirements + interest rate + loan amount range. (B) `__REDDIT__` >= 30 threads from /f/SmallBusiness, /f/Entrepreneurship, /f/Business, /f/startups, /f/Investing, /f/economics — discussions of loan application experiences, repayment challenges, and eligibility requirements. (C) `__WIKIPEDIA__` >= 25 articles, mandatory: United States Small Business Administration, Small business loan, Disaster loan, Microloan (United States), Working capital, Commercial real estate financing, Equipment financing, Line of credit, Loan amortization, Business plan — plus >= 15 more.

Output a loan catalog table with columns: loan type / terms / eligibility / interest rate / loan amount range / product URL (cited shopping) / community sentiment (cited reddit) / wiki reference URL. Add a 'loan eligibility decision tree': starting from the user's business size, industry, and credit history, walk to recommended loan types — each node cited. End with '5 loan types / products to AVOID and why' — each w/ shopping URL + reddit URL + wiki URL of the eligibility issue.

Format: markdown links. Begin with the catalog table. No chain-of-thought.
author_notes: Deep-tier task generated by gen_task_spec.py from configs/deep_topics/sba_loan_catalog.yaml. Golden: must=136, pool=510.
